section.title: NAME
section.p: split - At least two sharding groups environment,the data records will be cut into different sharding group according to the specified conditions.

section.title: DESCRIPTION
section.p: At least two sharding groups environment,the data records will be cut into different sharding group according to the specified conditions.

plentry.pd:
End of the range condition. (Optional, and only in the conditional cut time-effective, time-sharing a percentage cut invalid.)
Format Data spliting is divided into range-spliting and percent-spliting,"source shard" and "target shard"are common params,and are string type;the param "condition" and "endconditionis" filled when range spliting,it is a object of json structure;param "percent" is filled when percent-spliting,it is a int type. range-splitingRange Sharding use exact conditions when range-spliting, while Hash Sharding use "Partition"(number of slices) conditions. The starting condition is a required field and the ending condition is a optional condition when spliting, if the ending condition doesn't fill, then it default as the max range of data that "source shard" contains. ("<source shard>","<target shard>"，<condition>) percent-spliting db.foo.bar.split("<source shard>","<target shard>"，<percent>)
The range of partition, if you specify the partition key fields such as when descending:{groupingKey:{<field 1>:<-1>,condition (or Partition) the starting conditions range should be greater than the scope of the termination conditions.

body.section:
Examples
Range-spliting of Hash Sharding db.foo.bar.split("shard1", "shard2",{Partition:10},{Partition:20})
Range-spliting of Range Shardingdb.foo.bar.split("shard1", "shard2",{a:10}, {a:10000})
Percent-splitingdb.foo.bar.split("shard1", "shard2", 50)
